summary: Rebased FT fighter/core split work covering Kirby, Nana, Yoshi, CPU attack, collision, and metal helper matches. The PR reported successful `ninja changes_all` on the production baseline and FT matched/fuzzy improvements after removing an ftmaterial signature regression from the slice.

key_files.role: Listed in PR body and referenced by review for newly added pragmas near ftKb_Init_GetMotionFileString.
Kirby Marth/FE hat special-N matching changes; added setupStartAccessory inline helper, JObj include, external symbol, accessory setup changes, and hit capsule loop reshaping.

Kirby special-N/special-hi/special-lw matching changes; renamed locals, adjusted PAD_STACK/UNUSED locals, replaced ABS macro in one loop with explicit sign handling, and rewrote repeated assignments into chained assignments.
Nana init/collision matching improvements, including a full match for ftNn_Init_801230D0 according to bot report.
Yoshi special-S matching changes; review questioned replacing an apparent helper call with expanded body-like logic in ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll.
Fighter core matching improvements, including ft_80089B08 fuzzy improvement in bot report.
Fighter core matching changes; review flagged possible pragma cleanup near ftCo_800CDE74/ftCo_800CE650 area.
Fighter collision matching improvements for ftColl_8007BE3C, ftColl_8007861C, and ftColl_80079EA8 according to bot report.
Largest changed file in slice; CPU attack matching improvements across several ftCo_800Bxxxx functions according to bot report.
Metal helper matching improvement for ft_800C85B8 according to bot report.

what_changed: Refreshed FT fighter/core split work over Kirby, Nana, Yoshi, CPU attack, collision, and metal helper files.
Reported verification: slice was rebased onto origin/master f59b5e08b5c6553794378d9a5f640a0e58640f37 and `ninja changes_all` completed successfully on the production baseline worktree.
Bot report showed matched code improvement of +1716 bytes, 4 new matches, and 24 unmatched-item improvements.
New matches reported for ftNn_Init_801230D0, ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim, fn_8012EDE8, and ftkirbyspecialmars .sdata2.

ftkirbyspecialmars.c introduced `setupStartAccessory` to share accessory setup, changed hat access through `ft_80459B88.hats[...]`, used `HSD_JObjSetScale`, and rewrote two hit capsule loops using a `Fighter* hit_fp` byte-stride pattern.

ftkirbyspecialn.c adjusted local naming and stack/codegen steering in several Kirby functions, including `ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3570`, `ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialAirLw_Coll`, and `ftKb_EatWait_IASA`.
Yoshi special-S changes expanded wall-bounce effect logic in a collision function; this was explicitly questioned in review as possibly regressing away from an existing helper.
Review comments flagged pragmas in ftkirby.c and ft_0CDD.c, unresolved/new externs in ftkirbyspecialmars.c, and a possible helper-boundary regression in ftYs_SpecialS.c.

smart_moves.text: The PR isolated this FT slice and explicitly dropped an ftmaterial signature regression before reporting improvements, reducing the risk that unrelated signature churn polluted the slice.
The PR body recorded both baseline/rebase context and `ninja changes_all` verification, making the reported match improvements easier to interpret historically.
Creating `setupStartAccessory` in ftkirbyspecialmars.c suggests an attempt to restore a reusable helper boundary for repeated accessory setup rather than leaving all setup in the caller.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching slice introduces pragmas, externs, volatile locals, PAD_STACK, or dummy UNUSED storage, reviewers should require either cleanup or an explanation tied to matching evidence.

Do not remove or bypass an apparent local helper boundary merely for a local score change without explanation; reviewer feedback in this PR specifically questioned expanded Yoshi wall-bounce logic that stopped using an existing helper.
File-wide sweep is useful when a pattern appears multiple times: this slice had repeated pragma concerns in separate fighter files and repeated stack/local codegen steering in Kirby changes.

naming_conventions.text: The diff used local role names such as `pos_y`, `slide_speed`, `pos_x`, `top_y`, `bottom_y`, `floor_normal_x_sign`, `slide_angle`, and `abs_slide_angle` to replace less descriptive generated-style locals such as `vec`, `var_f3`, and `temp_f5` in Kirby special code.

The helper name `setupStartAccessory` is camelCase and locally scoped as inline in ftkirbyspecialmars.c; the PR evidence supports only that this name was introduced, not that it is a global naming convention.
The diff retained address-style or partially recovered names where semantics were not fully known, such as `fn_8010B2E8`, `ftKb_Init_803CB490`, `ftKb_Init_804D3E00`, and struct fields like `x88`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK and UNUSED dummy storage to influence stack layout in Kirby functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded standards require explicit verifier evidence and narrow scope for PAD_STACK/dummy locals; the PR body reports `ninja changes_all`, but the diff excerpt alone does not prove each tactic was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used `volatile f32 absX` in `ftKb_EatWait_IASA` as a codegen steering tactic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Volatile locals are a matching tactic requiring evidence; PR-level verification exists but no per-function rationale is present in loaded evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed repeated scalar/vector field assignments into chained assignments in Kirby special-lw collision paths.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Could improve local codegen/readability but should be checked against natural loop/source-shape expectations because the repeated pattern varies by index-like slots.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replaced ABS macro usage with explicit branch-based absolute value in `ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded standards prefer canonical macros such as ABS when they express source operation, unless assembly evidence supports explicit control flow.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced direct byte-stride pointer manipulation by casting a `Fighter*` through `u8*` and stepping by `sizeof(HitCapsule)` in two hit capsule loops.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Typed fields over pointer math standard warns against raw pointer arithmetic when a real type like `HitCapsule*` can express the access.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added or retained pragmas in fighter files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Review explicitly questioned these pragmas; loaded standards treat pragmas as exceptional and requiring tight scope/evidence.

review_feedback.feedback: Pragmas were flagged as possibly needing removal.
New externs should be resolved or explained.

Expanded Yoshi collision logic looked weird because a helper function appeared to exist but was no longer being used; reviewer asked to revert if this was a regression or explain why it had to remain.
Pragmas were flagged as possible cleanup targets.

follow_up_queries: ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC ABS macro explicit abs branch

notes: PR identity, author, state, body, verification text, review comments, and changed-file metadata are well supported.
The loaded raw diff is truncated; detailed claims are limited to visible hunks and bot report lines.

Metadata changed_files lists 9 files, while PR body and review comments also include `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirby.c`; this intake preserves the discrepancy rather than inventing a file-stat record for ftkirby.c.
Review comments were authored by fjooord and are treated as review feedback present in the slice, not as proof of final resolution.
The PR reported `ninja changes_all`, but no per-function objdiff or rationale is loaded for individual PAD_STACK, volatile, pragma, or pointer-math tactics.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Explain or remove exceptional codegen tactics in fighter matching slices
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2725 review flagged pragmas and unresolved externs, while the visible diff also used PAD_STACK, UNUSED dummy storage, volatile locals, and raw pointer stepping. Candidate lesson: fighter matching PRs should attach narrow evidence or cleanup notes when retaining these tactics.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve helper boundaries unless a regression explanation is provided
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2725 review explicitly questioned Yoshi special-S logic that appeared to stop using an existing helper, asking for revert or explanation if the expanded form was required.

source_update_candidates.title: Review comments reinforce requiring rationale for pragmas and externs in matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR2725 review comments asked to remove/fix pragmas in ftkirby.c and ft_0CDD.c and to resolve or explain new externs in ftkirbyspecialmars.c. This supports existing standards around exceptional pragmas and truthful declarations; curator may consider adding this PR as an example, not a new rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Source-owned standards already cover this pattern; PR provides a concrete historical example.

source_update_candidates.title: Helper-boundary regression example from Yoshi special-S
source_update_candidates.text: PR2725 review questioned expanding Yoshi wall-bounce logic where a helper function appeared available, asking for revert or explanation. Curator may attach this as an example under header/local helper boundary or inferred authored source style guidance.
source_update_candidates.reason: Good example of reviewer preference for preserving/restoring helper boundaries, but not enough evidence to create a new accepted standard by itself.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialmars.c touched hat accessory setup and hit capsule loops in PR2725
source_update_candidates.text: In PR2725, ftkirbyspecialmars.c added `setupStartAccessory`, used `ft_80459B88.hats[...]`, `ftCommon_SetAccessory`, `HSD_JObjSetScale`, and `lb_8000C2F8`; visible hunks also rewrote two Kirby Marth/FE special-N end hit capsule loops.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-scoped historical context for future work in this file; should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: ftYs_SpecialS.c helper-boundary review issue in PR2725
source_update_candidates.text: In PR2725, review flagged `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll` changes that appeared to bypass `ftYs_SpecialS_SpawnWallBounceEffect`, asking to revert or explain if required.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful future warning for this path, but current source should be checked before adding as an accepted path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all PR tactics were accepted just because the PR was merged; review comments show open concerns and the loaded slice does not show their resolution.
Do not promote `setupStartAccessory` camelCase as a global naming convention; evidence only shows one local helper name in this PR.
Do not claim `ninja changes_all` proves each PAD_STACK, volatile local, pragma, extern, or pointer cast was required; the PR provides only PR-level verification.
Do not invent changed-file stats for ftkirby.c; it is in the PR body and review comments but not in the loaded changed_files.jsonl metadata.
